parts.content:
Write 1 dissimilar SBA question(s) on: 'Left ventricular contractility — ejection fraction as best indicator'.

Each question should test application of knowledge, not simple recall — a candidate should need to reason from principles rather than just retrieve a fact. Use the retrieve tool to find relevant reference material before writing. Return the result as a QuestionSet.

parts.args: {"query": 'True contractility is difficult to measure clinically because most indices are load dependent.'}

{"topic": "Primary Frca Sample Sbas", "exam": "primary", "curriculum_node_code": null, "model": "openai:gpt-5.5", "generated_at": "2026-05-02T01:16:45.156172", "questions": [{"stem": "Myocardial contractility is the intrinsic ability of cardiac muscle fibres to do work at a given preload and afterload.", "lead": "Which measure is the best indicator of left ventricular contractility from the options listed?", "options": [{"text": "Ejection fraction", "is_correct": true, "explanation": "Ejection fraction is influenced by loading conditions, but among the listed variables it is the least dependent on preload and afterload and is the best overall indicator."}, {"text": "LV end diastolic pressure", "is_correct": false, "explanation": "LV end-diastolic pressure is strongly affected by preload and ventricular compliance, so it is not a good direct measure of contractility."}, {"text": "LV end diastolic volume", "is_correct": false, "explanation": "LV end-diastolic volume reflects preload and chamber compliance rather than intrinsic contractile state."}, {"text": "LV end systolic pressure", "is_correct": false, "explanation": "LV end-systolic pressure is influenced by afterload and arterial pressure and is not the best standalone measure of contractility."}, {"text": "Stroke volume", "is_correct": false, "explanation": "Stroke volume is heavily dependent on preload, afterload, heart rate, and contractility, so it is less specific than ejection fraction."}], "explanation": "True contractility is difficult to measure clinically because most indices are load dependent. Of the listed choices, ejection fraction is the best practical indicator, though it is still affected by preload and afterload.", "curriculum_node_codes": [], "curriculum_node_scores": [], "title": "Left ventricular contractility — ejection fraction as best indicator"}]}
